get.description: Always 200 when the process is reachable. `ready` mirrors `/ready`.
Returns 200 iff DB pool initialized and `SELECT 1` succeeded at startup.

200.description: Backend ready (DB pool up, SELECT 1 OK).

503.description: Not ready: DB pool down or initial ping failed.

post.summary: Login with email + password; sets td_session cookie.

get.summary: List empresas (scoped). Admin/ops see all; manager sees only theirs.
Manager: return your own empresa. 404 for admin/ops (no scope).

403.description: Out of scope for this user

get.summary: Counts of drivers, vehicles, contactos. Populated as later CRs add those tables.

403.description: Out of scope or insufficient role
Out of scope or insufficient role
Out of scope or insufficient role

post.summary: Regenerate the activation token + wa.me link. Invalidates previous token.

get.description: Aggregate report over every día of an empresa within [desde, hasta].

delete.description:
Delete a dia operativo.

Allowed only if:
  * Caller is `falabella_admin` or a `transport_manager` whose scope
    includes `dia.empresa_id` (ops cannot delete).
  * `dia.estado == 'BORRADOR'` — anything past validation is immutable
    (returns 409).

Cascade is explicit (no DB-level CASCADE existed pre-CR-021): we delete
visitas of the dia, then rutas of the dia, then the dia row itself.
Returns 204 No Content.

403.description: Out of scope or role not allowed to delete

409.description: Dia is not in BORRADOR; cannot delete

post.description:
Distribute `eta_estimada` across each route's visitas within a shift window.

Default mode (`respetar_reglas_cliente=false`) keeps the CR-019 behaviour:
even spacing of `duracion_horas / N` between visitas, ordered by
`visita.orden ASC` within each ruta.

With `respetar_reglas_cliente=true` (CR-028) the planner additionally:

  * Looks up each visita's `cliente_id` to fetch `ventana_horaria_inicio`,
    `ventana_horaria_fin`, `dias_no_disponible`, `prioridad`.
  * If the cliente is unavailable on the dia's weekday (ISO code in
    `dias_no_disponible`), the visita is SKIPPED (eta_estimada untouched)
    and a `PlanEtasWarning` is appended. We do NOT auto-cancel — that's
    the operator's call via POST /visitas/{id}/cancel.
  * If the proposed ETA falls outside the cliente's window, it is clamped
    to `ventana_horaria_inicio` of that same date (UTC).
  * If `prioridad in (1, 2)` and the visita is the first of its ruta, the
    ETA is pulled to `shift_start` regardless of orden gap. This is a soft
    nudge; subsequent visitas still use the orden-based offset.

Per affected visita we append `tipo='eta_recalc'` to the audit log with
`{eta_old, eta_new}` and (when applicable) the rule that nudged it.

patch.description:
Move a visita to a new position within its route.

Shifts the neighbours by ±1 so the resulting `orden` sequence within the
ruta remains a dense 1..N permutation. No-op if `nuevo_orden == orden`.

Allowed in BORRADOR / VALIDADO / EN_CURSO. CERRADO → 400.

post.description:
Mark a visita as cancelled with a motivo from the official catalog.

400 if `motivo_codigo` is not present (or inactive) in `td.motivos`.
409 if the visita is already in a terminal estado (entregado/no_entregado/
cancelado). 400 if the parent dia is CERRADO.

Renumber the pending visitas of a ruta so VIPs land at the head.

Completed/cancelled visitas keep their historical `orden` (we want the
timeline of what actually happened to remain stable). Pending visitas
(estado in {pendiente, en_camino}) get a fresh dense ranking, VIPs first
(preserving their relative `orden`), then non-VIPs.

Returns the count of VIPs whose position moved + the total touched.

Move a visita from its current ruta to another one in the SAME dia.

Steps (single transaction):
  1. Validate scope of the visita (via parent dia).
  2. Load the destination ruta and assert it belongs to the same dia.
  3. Compute `nuevo_orden`: if omitted, append (max(orden)+1).
  4. In origin ruta: shift `orden` down for those past `old_orden`.
  5. In destination ruta: shift `orden` up for those at/after `nuevo_orden`.
  6. Mutate the visita.
  7. Audit `tipo='ruta_change'`.

400 if either ruta is in a CERRADO dia. 400 if `nueva_ruta_id` belongs to
another dia.

get.description: Return the audit trail for one visita, most recent first.

post.description:
Advance simulation clock + interpolate driver positions.

Frontend calls this every few seconds while sim is running.
Server advances sim_now by (real_elapsed * speed) and moves drivers
along their routes proportional to sim_now between visita ETAs.

get.summary: Descarga plantilla XLSX para carga masiva.

post.summary: Carga masiva de empresas desde XLSX.
Carga masiva de conductores para una empresa.
Carga masiva de vehiculos para una empresa.
Carga masiva de contactos para una empresa.

get.summary: List clientes (scoped via visitas -> dias_operativos for transport_manager). Paginated wrapper: {items,total,limit,offset}.

parameters.name: empresa_id

schema.description: Filter clientes that have been served by this empresa (joins via visitas -> dias_operativos).
schema.title: Empresa Id

parameters.description: Filter clientes that have been served by this empresa (joins via visitas -> dias_operativos).

post.description:
Create a cliente. The master is tenant-agnostic — a brand-new cliente has
no visitas yet, so it has no derived empresa link until ingest connects it.

Idempotency on RUT is enforced by the global filtered unique index created
in CR-023 (rut IS NOT NULL UNIQUE). A duplicate RUT returns 409.

409.description: rut already exists (cliente master is global)

patch.summary: Update cliente master. CR-024: if `es_vip` changes, the new value is propagated to all active visitas (pendiente/en_camino in non-CERRADO days). `notas_operativas` changes are NOT denormalized — the LLM bot reads them live via `obtener_info_cliente_por_folio`.

delete.summary: Hard delete. Returns 409 if any visita references this cliente.

get.summary: Derived list of empresas that have served this cliente, computed live from visitas. Scoped to caller's empresa_ids when transport_manager.
Visit history for a cliente, scoped to caller's empresa_ids (transport_manager sees only their own visitas of that cliente).

post.summary: Bulk-cancel all pending / en_camino visitas of this cliente. Scope: `all` (default) | `today` | `next_n_days` (requires `dias`). Only affects visitas in BORRADOR/VALIDADO/EN_CURSO days.

get.summary: List pendiente/en_camino visitas for this cliente in the next N days (default 7). Restricted to BORRADOR/VALIDADO/EN_CURSO days. Scoped to caller's empresa_ids when transport_manager.

post.summary: Marcar/desmarcar 'No entregar' y avisar por WhatsApp al conductor.
Ingest Falabella source feed from XLSX (sheet 'Geo' preferred).
Ingest Falabella source feed from JSON body.
Trigger a one-shot Nominatim batch over pending clientes (admin).

parameters.name: empresa_id

schema.description: Scope to a single empresa. Omit to scan all pending across tenants.
schema.title: Empresa Id

parameters.description: Scope to a single empresa. Omit to scan all pending across tenants.

parameters.name: dia_id

schema.description: If set, resolves to that dia's empresa_id. Cannot combine with empresa_id mismatch.
schema.title: Dia Id

parameters.description: If set, resolves to that dia's empresa_id. Cannot combine with empresa_id mismatch.

parameters.name: max

schema.description: Cap on clientes processed this run (Nominatim is 1 req/s).
schema.title: Max

parameters.description: Cap on clientes processed this run (Nominatim is 1 req/s).

get.description:
List alerts, scoped to the caller's empresas.

By default hides alerts tied to a closed dia (CR-025) — when a dia
transitions to CERRADO every open alert on it is auto-resolved, but the
rows stay around for audit. Pass `incluir_cerradas=true` to include them
anyway. Alerts without `dia_id` (manual flags) are always shown because
they are not tied to the day lifecycle.

parameters.name: incluir_cerradas

schema.description: If False (default), excludes alerts whose parent dia is CERRADO. Manual alerts without dia_id are always included. Set True to see the full history regardless of dia state.
schema.title: Incluir Cerradas

parameters.description: If False (default), excludes alerts whose parent dia is CERRADO. Manual alerts without dia_id are always included. Set True to see the full history regardless of dia state.

patch.description: Transition estado to `resuelta` or `descartada`. Sets resolved_* fields.

post.description:
Create an operator-flagged alert. `tipo` is forced to 'manual'.

Roles allowed: admin / ops / transport_manager (with scope on
`body.empresa_id`). If `body.auto_dispatch` is True, fan out via
`dispatch_alert` and reflect the resulting state in the response.

Re-fan-out an alert. Admin only.

If the alert is `notificada` we revert it to `abierta` so the dispatcher's
idempotency guard doesn't skip it. resuelta/descartada are rejected.

Claim an alert ('Yo me encargo') so other operators see it's owned.
Passing it again by another user re-assigns ownership.

get.description:
Flat visita list ready to plot on the map.

Joins `visitas` → `dias_operativos` (for `empresa_id`, scope) → `rutas`
(for folio) → `empresas` (for `nombre`).

Scope behaviour:
  * `transport_manager` with `empresa_ids` that include outside-scope IDs
    gets only their own subset (silent filter, no 403).

parameters.name: empresa_ids

schema.description: CSV de empresa_id. Default: todas las accesibles al user.
schema.title: Empresa Ids

parameters.description: CSV de empresa_id. Default: todas las accesibles al user.

get.description:
Aggregate by comuna for HexagonLayer / heatmap rendering.

One row per comuna with: total / entregadas / no_entregadas / pendientes /
atrasadas / canceladas. Latitude/longitude are the comuna centroid from
`core.geocoding._COMUNA_CENTROIDS`. Comunas not present in the centroid
table are SKIPPED (the heatmap is best-effort visual; missing centroids
would render at lat=0,lon=0).

KPIs agregados para los paneles del mapa.

Returns global totals + per-empresa breakdown + top fail comunas + best/worst
ruta rankings (min 5 visitas to enter the ranking — small rutas have too
much variance to compare fairly).

Aggregated, role-scoped activation list. Conductores + contactos are
scoped to the actor's empresas; usuarios are included only for Falabella
admin/ops (platform users aren't empresa-scoped).

AlertCreate.title: AlertCreate
AlertCreate.description:
Body for POST /api/v1/alerts/manual.

`tipo` is hardcoded to `'manual'` server-side; we keep it out of the body
so a transport_manager can't sneak in a fake `eta_breach` and bypass
cron-side dedupe logic.

recipients.title: Recipients
recipients.description: Total recipients matched after severity/motivo filtering.

sent.title: Sent
sent.description: Recipients to whom send_whatsapp returned True.

AlertUpdate.title: AlertUpdate
AlertUpdate.description:
Body for PATCH /api/v1/alerts/{alert_id}.

Only `estado` transitions are allowed via PATCH. `resolved_at` and
`resolved_by_user_id` are derived server-side from current_user + now.

CancelPendingVisitasRequest.title: CancelPendingVisitasRequest
CancelPendingVisitasRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/v1/clientes/{id}/cancel-pending-visitas.

Scopes:
  * `all` — cancel every pendiente/en_camino across BORRADOR/VALIDADO/EN_CURSO
    dias for this cliente.
  * `today` — only dias whose `fecha == today`.
  * `next_n_days` — dias whose `fecha` is in [today, today+dias]. `dias`
    is required and bounded 1..30.

CancelPendingVisitasResult.title: CancelPendingVisitasResult
CancelPendingVisitasResult.description:
Result of a bulk cancel. `dia_ids` / `visita_ids` let the caller
invalidate caches on a per-day basis instead of re-fetching everything.

dias_no_disponible.title: Dias No Disponible
dias_no_disponible.description: ISO weekday codes the cliente cannot receive deliveries (mon..sun).

ClienteListResponse.title: ClienteListResponse
ClienteListResponse.description:
CR-023: paginated wrapper for list endpoints.

Response shape: ``{items, total, limit, offset}``.

dias_no_disponible.title: Dias No Disponible
dias_no_disponible.description: ISO weekday codes the cliente cannot receive deliveries (mon..sun).

ClienteVisitaProgramadaItem.title: ClienteVisitaProgramadaItem
ClienteVisitaProgramadaItem.description:
One row of the visitas-futuras endpoint. Mirrors historial but is
pendiente/en_camino-only and exposes the day's state so the UI can show
whether the visita is in a planning vs operational day.

DriverUpdate.title: DriverUpdate
DriverUpdate.description: PATCH — empresa_id cannot change here (admin DDL only).

EmpresaServidaOut.title: EmpresaServidaOut
EmpresaServidaOut.description:
One row of the derived "empresas servidas" projection for a cliente.

Computed live via:
    SELECT d.empresa_id, e.nombre,
           COUNT(v.visita_id), MIN(v.created_at), MAX(v.created_at)
    FROM visitas v
    JOIN dias_operativos d ON d.dia_id = v.dia_id
    JOIN empresas e ON e.empresa_id = d.empresa_id
    WHERE v.cliente_id = :id
    GROUP BY d.empresa_id, e.nombre

Scope-filtered to caller's `empresa_ids` when transport_manager.

EmpresaSummary.title: EmpresaSummary
EmpresaSummary.description: Stats card for an empresa. Counts populated as entities ship in later CRs.

FalabellaRow.title: FalabellaRow
FalabellaRow.description: One row of the Falabella source feed (1 row = 1 bulto).

fallback.title: Fallback
fallback.description: Left at comuna centroide for next pass

LoginResponse.title: LoginResponse
LoginResponse.description: Cookie is set in the response; the body returns the user payload.

atraso_min.title: Atraso Min
atraso_min.description: Minutos de atraso de una visita pendiente: max(0, sim_now - eta_estimada). null si no aplica.

OnTime.title: OnTime
OnTime.description: Punctuality over visitas that have both eta_estimada and completada_at.

PlanEtasResult.title: PlanEtasResult
PlanEtasResult.description:
POST /api/v1/operacion/dias/{dia_id}/plan-etas response.

Backwards compatible with the CR-019 shape (`visitas_planificadas`,
`shift_start`, `duracion_horas`). When `respetar_reglas_cliente=true` we
also return `warnings` describing visitas that were skipped because the
cliente is unavailable on the day's weekday.

PlanEtasWarning.title: PlanEtasWarning
PlanEtasWarning.description: One warning entry from POST /dias/{id}/plan-etas with reglas activas.

vips_promoted.title: Vips Promoted
vips_promoted.description: How many VIP visitas had their orden updated

visitas_reordered.title: Visitas Reordered
visitas_reordered.description: Total pending visitas whose orden was touched (VIPs + tail)

RangeReport.title: RangeReport
RangeReport.description:
Aggregate over every día of an empresa in [desde, hasta], plus a per-día
trend so the UI can chart the evolution across the range.

TrendPoint.title: TrendPoint
TrendPoint.description: One point in the per-día trend of a range report.

VehicleUpdate.title: VehicleUpdate
VehicleUpdate.description: All fields optional; cannot change empresa_id via PATCH (use admin DDL).

VisitaCancelIn.title: VisitaCancelIn
VisitaCancelIn.description:
POST /api/v1/operacion/visitas/{visita_id}/cancel body.

`motivo_codigo` must match a row in `td.motivos` (catalogo oficial). The
handler validates this and returns 400 otherwise.

VisitaEventoOut.title: VisitaEventoOut
VisitaEventoOut.description:
One row of `td.visita_eventos`.

`payload` is the parsed JSON from `payload_json`. We expose it as a dict
(or None) so the frontend doesn't have to re-parse.

VisitaMoveRouteIn.title: VisitaMoveRouteIn
VisitaMoveRouteIn.description:
POST /api/v1/operacion/visitas/{visita_id}/move-route body.

Origin and destination rutas must belong to the same `dia`. If `nuevo_orden`
is omitted, the visita is appended at the end of the destination route.
